title: Black Bean Quesadillas
description: These quick and easy black bean quesadillas are a family favorite, perfect for a weeknight meal. Packed with flavor and nutrients, they're sure to be a hit with both kids and adults.
instructions: Drain and rinse the black beans thoroughly.
In a medium bowl, mash about half of the black beans with a fork or potato masher. Leave the other half whole for texture.
Stir in the frozen corn, shredded cheddar cheese, cumin, chili powder, and salt into the black bean mixture.
Heat olive oil in a large non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Place one tortilla in the skillet. Spread about 1/4 cup of the black bean mixture over half of the tortilla. Fold the other half over to create a half-moon shape.
Cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until the tortilla is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Repeat with the remaining tortillas and filling.
Cut each quesadilla into wedges and serve immediately with salsa and sour cream, if desired.
tips: For picky eaters, you can finely chop some bell peppers or spinach and mix it into the black bean filling.
Adjust the spices to your family's preference. For a milder flavor, reduce the chili powder.
Serve with a side of sliced avocado or a simple green salad for a complete meal.
